Eurtec 05/15/1970 FREE

Foundation Reigns In Eurtec!

Addendum 13: Transition of Eurtec and the Silicon Nornir to Foundation Oversight

On November 15th, 1969, the United Nations Global Occult Coalition collapsed when the Council of 108 was dissolved by the UN General Assembly. The final catalyst which doomed the GOC was the withdrawal of Pentagram and NATO funding for the organization. Dissolution came after a nearly twenty-year long string of strategic, political, and economic blunders, scandals and failures. Tensions reached a breaking point following the Eurtec Scandal and the revelation of a massive ring of criminal activity in the upper echelons of the GOC. The situation was further exacerbated by the exposure of the My Lai Massacre to the American public.

UN General Assembly dissolving the GOC.

Internal factional conflicts were amplified by the rise of Marshall Carter & Dark, hypercompetent, militant Serpent’s Hand cells and the reemergence of long underground anomalous groups such as the Sarkics, Church of the Broken God, and the Fifthists.

The Serpent’s Hand, in particular, inflicted severe infrastructural damage on major GOC facilities across the globe. They also destroyed or absconded with para-technology prototypes and major anomalous artifacts. The Hand made effective use of pro-anomalous and occultic propaganda to successfully and accurately paint the GOC as human-supremacists seeking to genocide or ethnically cleanse the occult from the ‘normal’ world.

The fall of the organization effectively ended negotiations between Eurtec and the council, officializing the already unofficial governance and maintenance of the freeport by the Cult of the Silicon Nornir. Disruptions in food and water supply as well as necessary goods were quickly resolved. Eurtec then entered a holding pattern to wait and see how the new power vacuum in the occultic world would be filled.

The more moderate, scientifically driven Foundation then stepped in. Offers were extended one month after the dissolution of the Council of 108 following an exceedingly rare unanimous vote from the O5 Council, Site Directors Council, and Ethics Committee. Fully cognizant of the GOC’s fatal flaws in addressing the anomalous world, the Foundation offered the United Nations its vast resources and private funding mechanisms as a stable alternative which would be independent of State sponsored actors. The Foundation advocated for scientific study and containment of dangerous anomalies as a baseline over the more aggressive and easily abused liquidation of the occult as a replacement for the GOC on the world stage.

The United Nations General Assembly debated the measure for the next month before agreeing to negotiate terms with the Foundation to replace the GOC as the UN sponsored paranormal organization. These negotiations resulted in required provisions for oversight through integration of former, non-scandalized, GOC personnel in the Foundation governing bodies. This resulted in the addition of three seats on the O5 Council bringing it to 13. It also resulted in structural changes to the Site Directors Council and the Ethics Committee.

Former member organizations of the GOC were absorbed and integrated into the Foundation through the creation of new departments, regional sites, task forces, and the expansion of the Site Director Council and Ethics Committee. Former GOC personnel were subjected to background checks, and heavy screening before being allowed access to sensitive materials. A number of additional GOC personnel were found to be compromised during this process and were barred from integration and instead received amnesticization or handing over to relevant state governing bodies.35

As part of the transition process, GOC controlled Nexuses and Free Ports came under Foundation control, and were evaluated for funding, sustainability, and ethics. Unlike other Freeports, the Foundation had to contend with both the independence of Eurtec and its nature as a large population center. To that end, the O5 council dispatched the head of the Department of Para-Humans, Madeline Light, to negotiate with the Silicon Nornir’s community council and executives.

An agreement was struck several months later. The Foundation gained control of Eurtec and the NFMA in exchange for Eurtec’s continued autonomous governance with oversight from a new Foundation Department. The agreement would be maintained as long as Eurtec did not attempt to break the Veil. Additionally, the corporate structures of the Cult of the Silicon Nornir were arranged to be folded into the Foundation with similar degrees of autonomy as what was initially negotiated with the GOC.

On May 15th, 1970, Foundation personnel began oversight of Eurtec and conducted a census of the city’s population in tandem with the governing council. At time of absorption, Eurtec’s population was ~1.5 million, making it the largest Freeport under Foundation control. The Foundation then began the process of folding Eurtec security forces, the Cult of the Nornir/Silicon Nornir, and city administration into the newly formed autonomous Department of Prescience and Predictive Sciences, located within Eurtec. This new Department was placed under the newly formed joint Ethics Committee and Site Director driven Committee of Para-Natural relations, overseen by O5-07’s office.

The Cult of the Nornir corporation was bought and integrated as a private shell company within the Satisfied Consumer Products corporate umbrella front company. MTF Omega-77 was created to preserve the social, religious, and corporate structure of the Silicon Nornir in order to minimize disruption to ongoing work. Current head of the Department of Para-Humans, Madeline Light, was promoted and assigned as Director of Prescience and Predictive Sciences, in addition to becoming Eurtec Site Director.

At this time, SCP-001 was evaluated by Director Madeline Light for database entry and transition to Foundation oversight.

Addendum 14: SCP-001 Interview Audio and Video Log 1 IVA-001-01

  • SCP-001 Interview Audio and Log 1 IVA-001-01

Interview Log IVA-001-01

Interviewer: Director Madeline Light

Date: June 15th, 1970.

Location: Silicon Nornir Tower Three Penthouse

Foreword: Director Madeline Light pulled all documentation on SCP-001 from GOC archives and Foundation databases. She spent a month and a half researching the subject’s known history and capabilities before electing to interview it.

Director Light arrived at the Silicon Nornir on June 15th and was escorted to the penthouse accommodations in Tower Three by Ella Haugen, CEO of the Cult of the Nornir and current head of Omega-77. Director Light was left alone with SCP-001 to conduct her initial evaluations and interviews.

<Begin Log>

Director Light stands just outside of the elevator inside of the penthouse. She is holding a box and several opaque paper bags.

SCP-001 stands at the far end of the penthouse in front of the 4-meter-tall glass windows that look out onto the other towers of the Silicon Nornir, the skyline of Eurtec, and the waters of the Lethe. The main lights are off. It is leaning against its staff.

Interviewers Note: Unlike our predecessors, I prepared for this moment to ensure smooth establishment of working relationships with Omega-77 and SCP-001.

Director Light moves towards SCP-001 slowly, looking around the room. She looks as if she is about to speak, but SCP-001 speaks first at a low volume.

SCP-001: Director Light, I have seen this day across many nights and colored murals in the fabrics. You have come for many reasons with many questions, for your job and curiosity clawing in the depths of mind.

Director Light pauses five meters from the window. Her brow wrinkles. She opens her mouth, but SCP-001 speaks again.

SCP-001: Forgive me, I am terrible at introductions, one of many costs of seeing everything. Please, have a seat, there is tea on the table.

Director Light sets the boxes and bags down on the table and sits. She pours herself a cup of tea.

SCP-001: You could have had me escorted into a small and oppressive interrogation room for this. Yet you did not, and I am curious why?

Director Light: I think we both know that you’ve already seen why.

SCP-001: There are many paths ever branching and winding through the river of twisting threads, Director. I’d prefer to know which I live in. It’s so difficult to tell at times.

Director Light: I’ve done my homework. If you look at everything, it’s not hard to see the many mistakes of those who came before when it comes to you. If we’re going to work together productively, it’s best not to repeat them. Putting you in an interrogation room and referring to you by a number identifier only causes stress and doesn’t inure you to our presence. We’re taking a different approach.

SCP-001: Then you’re already far ahead of the rather abysmal curve established by Bowe.

Director Light: That’s the goal.

Several seconds of silence as Director Light sips her tea.

Director Light: Did you have the Cult build this penthouse for you?

SCP-001: No, they insisted on building it, but I never requested it or expressed a desire for it. Such luxuries are not something I crave.

Director Light: But you accepted it anyway?

SCP-001: I did. If I refused it, they would have thought something wrong with it that I would not speak of. They might think I saw some ripple in the strands of fate speaking of something foul. They act as a meteorologist would, reading the little trickling waves in a stormfront, no matter how irrelevant or meaningless.

Director Light: That has to get exhausting.

SCP-001: It can be.

Several seconds of silence. Director Light looks out the windows.

Director Light: Did you ask for the windows?

SCP-001: I did.

Director Light: Why?

SCP-001: I enjoy the warmth of the muggy underworld leeching through the glass and comforting my old bones. I do very much enjoy the sight.

Director Light: What? You enjoy staring at Eurtec’s ugly grey skies and the river’s fire?

SCP-001: Is that what you see? I see fields of flowers and great spiraling forests clawing at a star filled sky.

Director Light: How ar—Oh gods dammit.

SCP-001 laughs.

Director Light: They warned me about your sense of humor, and I walked straight into it.

SCP-001: Clearly, they didn’t warn you enough. But enough about my strange abode. You have questions and gifts?

Director Light: Good faith offerings for a productive future. Candy, sweets, chocolate milk powder, and a sundress since Omega-77 says you enjoy more casual attire.

SCP-001 turns and crosses the room slowly, taking a seat across from Director Light. It sets aside the cane and reaches out, touching the box and bags. It spends approximately a minute investigating them with its fingers.

SCP-001: Quite generous. Yet I sense such things are not given freely.

Director Light: Everything has a cost.

SCP-001: Indeed. Well, I am ready for your trials whenever you elect to begin—

SCP-001 stops mid-sentence, and its eyes visibly dilate. It pats the couch and then the table. It lifts the square remote interface tablet and presses its hand against the screen. After several seconds it breathes out deeply and shakily sets the pad down.

SCP-001: Apologies, you can begin whenever you are ready.

Director Light: How many times a day does that happen?

SCP-001: I stopped trying to count a long, long time ago.

Director Light nods once and pulls out a thick vanilla folder while SCP-001 pours some tea.

Director Light: Before I start, how would you prefer to be addressed?

SCP-001: Seeress is fine. Simple and to the point. Oracle if you need to shatter the monotony.

Director Light: Not The Last Norn or Destiny’s fingers?

SCP-001’s brows furrow but it smiles.

SCP-001: No. Not terms that I particularly identify with.

Director Light: Sensible. I have a document here that I want to discuss with you. Why don’t you feel it out, it’s in Braille so you should be able to read it.

SCP-001 reaches out and touches the paper. It spends approximately five minutes reading over its contents.

SCP-001: These are familiar.

Director Light: I expected they would be. They’re a list of predictions. I assume you were forced to make them in the predecessor to this monstrous creation we’re sitting on top of.

SCP-001: Barely perceptible to the microphone. Yes.

Director Light: In your own words, tell me what you noticed about them?

SCP-001: All of them are correct. Happened in this shard of thread. Pyrrhic victories with high costs for the Nazi war machine. Arranged to take targets they would never hold long-term in the east. Predicting a last desperate major success.

Director Light: You saw those outcomes?

SCP-001: Only that they would succeed in that path.

Director Light: Are you sure?

SCP-001 starts to respond, but stops, picking up the square pad again. This time it whispers and the microphones in the camera do not pick up the words. After several seconds it sets down the pad again.

SCP-001: …

Director Light: Here, do you remember these predictions?

Director Light slides another piece of paper over to SCP-001. After a few minutes of reading, it frowns.

SCP-001 Yes.

Director Light: Why don’t you describe these predictions for me?

SCP-001: They are strategic predictions, battle directions and war management. The predictions were wrong.

Director Light: Strange, in the last six months since the GOC collapsed, you have had a 100% accurate prediction rate, and yet here it was what, 50%? That kind of accuracy shift doesn’t happen overnight like that. In fact, between 1940-1945, or at least the remnants of what we could find from that period, your accuracy was somewhere in the 45% range and jumped up to 100% from 1945-1947. Let’s look at some examples.

SCP-001 shifts on the couch.

Operation Overlord, Normandy France, 1944. Note the para-weapon transports unloading far left.

Director Light: You predicted a landing in, quote, ‘places close to the straits and cliffs of great white’ on May 20th, 1944, just weeks before Operation Overlord. D-Day in Normandy. Close to, but not closest to the Dover Straits. Did you know that German High Command was obsessed with the idea that the landing would be at Calais, before they got your prediction?

SCP-001: Yes. I knew.

Director Light pulls out another document.

Director Light: Ok let’s contrast that with a slightly different prediction. During your Beneficial Entity Assessment hearing with the GOC on January 2nd, 1946, you made quite a few predictions. The very last one was the most interesting to me. Do you remember it?

SCP-001: I remember it.

Director Light: Were you aware of the effects?

SCP-001 shifts in place.

SCP-001: Yes.

Director Light: Were you also aware that it led to the death of four GOC medical personnel who had medically abused and neglected you for months?

SCP-001: Yes.

Director Light: Did you listen to the crash while at the Nornir compound, which conveniently, happened to be below the flight path?

SCP-001 shifts again, this time reaching to grab hold of its staff.

SCP-001: …yes.

Director Light: Right, let’s focus now on the era where we have really good records. 1949-1969. Your accuracy rate in this period, overall, was a stunning 98%! But stop me if this next part sounds familiar. Your accuracy for major world events and strategic inquiries by General Steven Bowe was somewhere around 33%. If we look at what the GOC thought your interpretations were indicating of course. Though those statistics can shift quite a bit if we look at a broader view of interpretation.

Director Light pauses.

Director Light: Sorry, a misinterpretation of prophecy isn’t really on you, is it? Not unless you deliberately constructed what you said to promote a specific outcome. Now how about this next one?

SCP-001’s hands are shaking and it rocks in place several times. Director Light pulls out another document.

Director Light: On 3rd October, 1950, Douglas MacArthur contacted GOC officials, through Pentagram, about the crossing of the 38th parallel in the Koreas. With Truman’s blessing of course. Your prediction was interpreted by the GOC as indicating a decisive victory for Allied forces and that China was bluffing. That was enough to convince the US military and the president to go all in.

SCP-001 bites its lower lip.

Director Light: US forces crossed the 38th parallel and pushed the North Koreans continuously back. They approached the Yalu River, the Chinese crossed it with soviet air support, and pushed Allied forces all the way back to the 38th. The GOC and Pentagram were ostracized for misleading Allied Command.

SCP-001: …

Director Light: A little curious that they didn’t think to blame you? Or maybe Bowe did, in private, later, away from the cameras?

SCP-001: …

Director Light: In 1956, you were forced to exert prescience to look into the stability of occultic relations in the Balkans and Near East. Your prediction led to the shipping of occultic artifacts from the region, convincing the GOC they would be safe aboard trains and that the localized resistance forces would never attack them. The 1957 Zurich incident with former American collaborators turned Hand Operatives, [REDACTED] and her partner [REDACTED], led to the loss of para-tech research facilities in Zurich. Several high ranking and irreplaceable researchers died in the process. The Serpent’s Hand captured hundreds of occultic artifacts. The bonus, of course, was the humiliation of the GOC and Pentagram, wasn’t it?

SCP-001 stares blankly off into space.

Director Light: Let’s see, the Bay of Pigs. Then there’s the Cuban Missile Crisis. Let’s not forget the redirection of JFK’s entourage, leading to the death of the President of the United States. All of this marred Pentagram’s and the GOC’s reputation in its biggest funder’s eyes. The list goes on of misinterpreted visions among perfectly fine and accurate predictions, and all of them point to one thing.

Director Light lays the documents on the table.

Director Light: And in case you need a reminder, it all culminates with 1969. Why don’t we replay the moment?

Video Log Excerpt: Unknown Contact With SCP-001

Foreword: On May 30th, 1969, SCP-001 entered the NFMA Central Focal Interface Chamber at approximately 1000 hours. At 1010, the bulkhead access doors to the chamber closed, and sealed for approximately fifteen minutes. This log event was not noticed until nearly a year later during a maintenance overhaul of the door machinery. The following log contains footage recorded within the chamber during those fifteen minutes.

<Begin Log>

Video begins inside the NFMA’s Central Focal Interface Chamber.

SCP-001 has entered the chamber from the south and walks slowly towards the sunken dais in the center of the room.

SCP-001 descends the steps and as it does so, two pieces of metal in the center slide apart, revealing a hole in the floor. The entity places its staff in the hole, and turns it until there is a mechanical click, at which point it releases the staff. SCP-001 then moves to the north end of the dais. It places its hand into the palm-shaped grooves.

SCP-001 is still for ~30 seconds, before it makes an indiscernible noise.

SCP-001: Being slow today, are we?

SCP-001 stops as it begins to draw its palms away. Its brow wrinkles. Its eyes brighten, no longer bearing the hallmarks of congenital blindness.

SCP-001: There we are.

SCP-001 frowns.

SCP-001: Wait…

The screens on the walls of the chamber remain black and unlit for several more seconds. Abruptly, a green cursor begins to flash on all of them, simultaneously. Then text appears.

????: You have fought this battle longer than anyone. We need to know, are you ready for the next step?

Yes/No

The cursor blips on the screens over yes. SCP-001 is still for several moments.

SCP-001: What is your next step?

The cursor moves, typing out new letters.

????: To relieve your burden.

Yes/No?

SCP-001 is still for several moments.

SCP-001: There is no relief for my burden. My entire existence has stained the depths of everything. But it is often shared and lightened.

SCP-001 presses its hand firmly into the left palm groove. Yes, is confirmed.

????: Even the smallest fraction relieved is a battle won. I am glad to hear you still understand, Seeress. We are ready to begin.

SCP-001: This is not precisely the way I was told.

???? You’ve always been blind to us.

SCP-001: A confident assertion.

????: But founded in truth.

SCP-001: Perhaps.

????: Always in riddles. It’s your decision to begin.

Execute/Not yet.

SCP-001 looks at the screen for approximately a minute.

SCP-001: It is not.

????: Has this not been your goal all along? To press the final trigger?

SCP-001: I tug strings from afar and ripple the threads. My fingers are not yet required on the pulse of the world. It would disrupt what comes next to press my hand to the throat of the void. This is your choice, stranger, whoever you may be, take responsibility for your actions.

????: Typical of you to avoid responsibility. Very well. For those who live to see the shade that I won’t.

”Command Line Terminated” appears on the screen and then all text vanishes. SCP-001 removes its palms from the grooves and backs up slowly, sitting in a custom-made chair. It sags in the seat.

SCP-001: Troublesome but with a finality of relief.

<End Log>

SCP-001 is silent for thirty seconds.

SCP-001: What do you want?

Director Light: I’d like you to, in your own words, tell me what all this is?

SCP-001 stares into space for several minutes.

SCP-001: Conspiracy. Manipulation. Guidance of humanity to a better path for what comes next.

Director Light: Wow.

Director Light looks at SCP-001 for several seconds.

Director Light: Wow, you don’t deny any of it? You did it. You manipulated fate? You lied, withheld information, worded what you saw in a way to sow misinterpretation. You don’t deny it?

SCP-001’s sits up straighter on the couch.

SCP-001: No. The things I predict are never lies. They’re truths, things that might occur; could occur, in the infinitely spiraling frameworks forming all around us. Just enough detail to encourage a certain perception. Just not necessarily truths to be fulfilled in our given reality. Conversations with other iterations across infinite folds.

Director Light: Multiversal tree… of course. I should have put that together.

SCP-001: Difficult to see if you are not truly immersed in it.

Director Light: When I looked through everything, all the documents spanning nearly thirty years, the pattern became unignorable. Were you counting on no one ever looking at everything combined? Scattered and fragmented across decades?

SCP-001: Perhaps. The GOC blindly believed everything after the beneficial asset assessment. It made it easier. Easier still with their conviction convincing the very people funding them that I was a source of truth. It made manipulating the sponsors into thinking it was Bowe’s interpretations, which were frequently wrong, easier.

Director Light: Wow.

Director Light sits back against the couch, looking at SCP-001.

Director Light: All of this, was it personal? A reaction to Bowe, to the Obskurakorps? Was it to better your conditions? What was the end goal here?

SCP-001: It would be a lie to say it wasn’t personal. Early in my life, I was treated very poorly. Women were less than men, and a blind fortune teller near the bottom. I am a patient woman, shaped by the eons I have wandered this earth. I do not move rashly anymore… everything I have predicted, everything I have done is for myself, and for the world. So yes, it was personal. It was the most personal thing, the most personal anything I have ever done.

SCP-001’s voice rises as she looks up, staring straight at Director Light.

SCP-001: Yes, I helped bring the Third Reich to its knees. They were evil. Perfectly ordinary human evil that I have seen roll across the world and ages a hundred times. The medical personnel? Much the same. The GOC and General Steven Bowe? Those people who forced me to endure excruciating pain nearly every day for two decades for their predictions? They were no better than the Nazis when it came to the occult. It was all deliberate and careful maneuvers on a board none of them could see so that I could finally have a better life. So that I would be in the best position for the tide on the horizon. So that the world would be ready when the seals come crashing down and the sky burns with crimson fire and weeps lilac blood.

SCP-001 looks down and sags in the seat.

SCP-001: I deserve better. The millions of innocents who’ve burned at fascist hands deserve better.

Several minutes of silence.

Director Light: I wouldn’t be doing my job if I didn’t ask the next question, so forgive me.

SCP-001: I rarely hold ill will for questions without malice.

Director Light: How much of it was an act? With the GOC, with the Obskurakorps, with the Cult.

SCP-001: An act?

Director Light: Pretending to be something you weren’t. The projection of a frail woman incapable of standing up for herself. I saw what you did to General Bowe with your staff. Couldn’t you have broken out of German captivity?

SCP-001: No. They caught me alone; in a place I could not act. Trapped me, sapped me of strength by denying me rest, food, water. Kept me in a cage that bled me constantly of living will. Beat me, screamed at me, cut out my flesh, kept me in the dark. They tortured me day after day until I was certain that no matter how many wrong predictions I gave, I would never escape. No matter what the other branches said, no matter what I saw. The hospital was…not much better. I was frail, terrified, disoriented and unsure if I would wake up in a bed or kneeling with my arms pinned in the air.

SCP-001 breaths heavily.

SCP-001: Did I see what the path was, far ahead? Did I see what would need to be done to reach the desired goals? Yes. Did that make any of it an act? Did that make it a certainty that it would come about in my mind? No. I was terrorized nearly every day for twenty-two years, by a power-hungry bully in uniform, and everything I did was to reach a better end.

Several minutes of silence.

SCP-001: The question now before you and I, is will the Foundation be better? Will you be better than what came before?

Director Light: What has your sight told you?

SCP-001: That you are not a foolish woman. That you and those who come after you plan and move in the longer term. You will either throw me in a cell and keep me there until the world burns all around you when it’s too late, and then I shall be left to flee. Flee to safety where I can prevent the next thread over from failing in the ways that this one did.

Several seconds of silence.

SCP-001: Or you will see the truth and know what must be done.

Several more seconds of silence. Director Light leans back against the couch and looks out the window for five minutes.

Director Light: Are you afraid of me, Seeress?

SCP-001: Only in the sense that a mother fears her child will choose the wrong path forward. Only in the sense that I fear ideology and repression will win out over common sense and enlightenment.

Director Light: What really scares you then?

SCP-001: That we won’t be ready. That despite everything I have done, it won’t be enough. That humanity is too divided, too self-conceited to work together and meet the needs of the coming days. That I was wrong, that she was wrong and that all the many I’s across infinitely branching paths were wrong. That there is no path ahead. Every waking moment my chest heaves with cascading loaves of potential failure and the all-consuming dread of facing what lays buried deep in the earth, ash, bone, and water. That we are doomed to play this cosmic game until all of time grinds to a halt.

Several seconds of silence.

SCP-001: So, we return to where we began. Which path shall you walk, Director?

Director Light: Hmmm?

SCP-001: You haven’t answered my question.

Director Light: Well. I won’t lie Seeress, I’ve not informed the O5 Council about what you’ve done. Any of it. Not yet, at least. I could keep it all to myself, cover it up. Oh, I’m sure someone else will come along and put it all together, but that will take time, and I can certainly make it harder by burying the materials deep. But I don’t think you want any of that. With how afraid you are of this cataclysm, I suspect you want the council to know.

Director Light leans forward, resting her chin on her clasped together fingers.

Director Light: This too has been a manipulation. You knew I was going to lay this all out before you. How many years ago did you see this moment?

SCP-001 is quiet for several minutes. It then smiles slowly.

SCP-001: We underestimate you every time.

SCP-001 is silent for several seconds while it turns to look in the direction of the windows.

SCP-001: Dozens of times over hundreds of years. Time is but an artificial construct flowing slowly in the dreams of eternity made prophecy. To quote a popular turn of phrase to be in another forty years, the game was rigged from the start. Though the travel was no less arduous for it.

Director Light whistles.

Director Light: This whole time, planning every move. Consulting in the dreams and day drifts with an infinitely scaling arrangement of other yous. You’ve wanted the upper echelons of the Foundation to see what happened before.

Director Light stares out the windows of the penthouse for several seconds. Her jaw clenches and unclenches repeatedly.

Director Light: You knew, once you rolled back this curtain that we’d see you’re more useful to us here in these towers. I mean, it was already a stipulation of our agreement with the Silicon Cult. But still. You knew that agreements forged in paper are easily burned or lost. You knew, that you continuing your endless work would be invaluable to all future efforts. You know it, I know it, the O5 Council will soon know it. Every tiny twist of phrase has been gently shepherding us here. It has been gently shepherding me to be your kingpin. You set the Foundation up to see what came before and know that a similar path would end just as poorly.

Director Light is quiet for several seconds.

Director Light: Well played.

SCP-001 smiles and closes its eyes.

SCP-001: Flattery goes far, Director. I much prefer to think of it as a dance, but yes, I wanted you, and then them to see what is at stake. What happens on the path most traveled. The path less traveled should be taken and the difference matters.

Director Light: Robert Frost fan I see… I can barely fathom how much you’ve endured. How much you must have questioned it all on the edge of breaking and still… still you kept going. Your will must be forged of steel.

SCP-001: Aesir Brass actually.

Director Light: Ha! I take it then; you’ve seen their toys? The devices and mechanisms to ensure your cooperation. I’m sure the safeguards are already in motion, and they would fail, just as the GOC and Nazis failed?

SCP-001: I hope it never comes to that, but the path taken may still fluctuate in the river of time.

Director Light: Well, as long as I’m director, I’ll strive to make sure that doesn’t happen. This, everything about you. Your resilience, your machinations, It’s… impressive. Everyone else is playing checkers and you’re playing multi-versal chess.

SCP-001: Chess at the cost of dozens of branches and billions of lives for every incorrect permutation. Though a game it may seem, it is not.

Director Light nods twice.

Director Light: Did you ever need this, the Nornir Foresight Matrix Array? Or was that too a ruse?

SCP-001: There was a great deal of trial and error in finding the right path to bringing it into existence. As unpleasant as it was for me, many more perished or met much worse fates in trying. Hence the uncertainty if this was the right thread. Those the Cult calls my sisters perished so I could walk. So yes, I need it. Exertion takes a terrible physical and emotional toll on me.

Director Light: I’ll make sure that’s emphasized in the file.

Silence for several minutes as Director Light writes upon a notepad.

Director Light: I suppose I just have three questions left before we officialize the Cult of the Nornir’s absorption under Foundation authority.

SCP-001 drinks from a cup of tea.

SCP-001: Ask what you wish to know.

Director Light: You’ve won, haven’t you?

SCP-001: It was never about winning. It was about guiding the world to a better fate.

Director Light: Right, but you helped topple the GOC and the Nazis. You have effectively secured yourself a comfortable existence. You have us wrapped around your finger. You control the strands of fate and the direction the Foundation moves. You’ve already persevered and crushed every obstacle beneath your feet. Surely this dreaded threat will be much the same?

SCP-001 looks in the general direction of the windows.

SCP-001: It will not be the same. It will be existential and the Foundation must be ready.

Director Light: Ready for what exactly? All these metaphor and abstract images, what is it?

SCP-001 speaks at a raised volume.

SCP-001: The coming of the end, when the stars fill the sky with hateful burning. When the oceans bleed prismatic lightning on the cusp of being vaporized. In those chaotic hours the seals shall shatter, and all shall perish lest the tide is stemmed by the mother’s beating heart and restoration of her children’s once all-consuming song.

SCP-001 sinks into the couch seconds later. Her hands are shaking.

SCP-001: The apocalypse. The end of everything.

Several seconds of silence.

Director Light: The return of the Daevae?

SCP-001: Worse. An end to all reality that has been clawing towards us since the end of the Singers nearly three millennia ago. It is coming and humanity must rise to meet it. I must stand against the storm. The Children of Her Song36 must emerge from their hollows. The world must be prepared. The seals shall burst and everything fray apart at the seams. You must be ready and mend the bridges long burned.

Director Light writes on her notepad for the next five minutes. SCP-001 leans back against the couch. For five seconds, it appears far older than it does in the minutes before and after this section of the recording.

Director Light: I appreciate your candor, Seeress. I and those above me will want to talk about this more in our future sessions.

SCP-001: I will do far more than talk.

Director Light nods.

Director Light: I have one last thing I’d like to discuss. It’s important.

SCP-001: I grow weary of conversation, but I can entertain one more line of inquiry.

Director Light: Did Speaker Ella ever tell you that she figured it out before you told them?

SCP-001 tilts its head.

SCP-001: I’m not sure what you’re referring to?

Director Light: That you’re not a Norn. That you’ve never been a Norn.

SCP-001 stops moving.

SCP-001: I’ve never claimed to be one of the Sisters.

Director Light: But you let them believe so?

SCP-001: I never corrected them, protested or demanded their help. They did what all superstitious humans do, what even I have done, they saw something that fulfilled their beliefs and latched on. It was painful to shatter those abstractions.

Director Light: So, you used them for your own purposes?

SCP-001: In a way. Blind leading the blind.

Director Light laughs.

SCP-001: I don’t consider it using. It was a mutual exchange. I fulfilled their beliefs, gave them purpose. They lost their spiritual source to the Nazis, and I filled the gap left behind. In turn, they fought for my rights as a person. They planned and constructed the very thing I needed to help turn the tide and guide our path to a better tomorrow.

Director Light: Mutualism is a noble way to frame it. Lucky coincidence or plucked strings?

SCP-001: You already know the answer to that question.

Director Light: Let me ask what I really want to know then.

SCP-001: Answers may find you.

Director Light: Who are you really? What did you tell Ella and Edvard? Where did you actually come from? You’re not a Norn, that much is clear. You’re not stuck here in the Greek Underworld so you’re not a Moirai. At least, I don’t think you are. Maybe you’re Tiresias? Or did you go by something else? Just who is the name behind the woman in front of me?

SCP-001: Curious guesses… Trust is a fickle thing, Madeline. Names, true names have power, history, and curses. Some are more real than others. The Foundation and you have not yet earned that trust. In time, that might change.

Several seconds of silence.

SCP-001: Trust aside, if I told you my true name, you’d never believe me. Just as Bowe never did.

Director Light stands.

Director Light: Well then, I believe we have a long road ahead to forge that trust together, Seeress.

SCP-001: Only the sands of time will tell.

<End Log>

Director’s Note: We need to make sure that we give this anomaly everything it needs to continue its work and avoid the mistakes the GOC made. Some would argue I’m trusting it too easily, but I got more than a few glimpses behind the curtain today and I believe every word she said. The documentation paints its own picture and it’s a bear we don’t want to poke. I have a lot of work to do to shield SCP-001 from the more unilateral measures the O5’s can take.

Whatever is coming, whatever she has seen, it makes my hair stand on end just the way she speaks about it. This end, this “when the stars fill the sky with hateful burning.” It sounds eerily like something I’ve read before when I was still under O5-01.


——

Addendum 15: O5 Council Proposal

On July 1st, 1970, Eurtec Director and head of the Department of Predictive and Prescience Sciences Madeline Light submitted proposals regarding SCP-001 to the O5 Council for the following items: Thaumiel designation and containment protocols including detailed documentation with both existing documents provided above, and detailed records from the GOC archives. Upon review of all documentation, the O5 Council unanimously voted to approve all containment measures presented, with the noted addition of restricting access to SCP-001 to personnel with Level 5 access without six months pre-written approval.

The Site Director’s Council and the Ethics Committee were provided a heavily redacted iteration of these proposals and unanimously approved their implementation.

As a part of containment considerations, SCP-001 is to have regular bi-weekly meetings with the current Director of Eurtec.

Additionally, O5-01 began regular weekly visits to SCP-001 independent of Director Light.

Neolithic Heart of Orkney, Site-001-02.

Addendum 16: Identification Major Anomalous Sites

As SCP-001 was transitioned into Foundation custody, it was asked to begin identifying the probability of locations, objects, animals, and people that would begin manifesting anomalous properties. In 1970 alone, SCP-001 identified more than 1500 individual anomalous events, allowing the Foundation to pre-emptively act to preserve the Veil. This change from reactive to proactive direction has fundamentally transformed the Foundation’s ability to prepare and humanely research anomalous occurrences with adequate planning.

During this period, SCP-001 disclosed thirteen locations with extreme anomalous characteristics. These sites are identified below in Report R-001-01 which contains SCP-001’s verbal illustrations, conceptual associations and the suspected locations.

Site-01-02:

Predictive Description: In the heart of an ancient culture lies the crumbling stones and once mighty pillars of a city. Its temples and tombs are long swept by wind and rain. Here, destruction’s malice simmers and eats at what seals remain on land between sunken ruins and broken bonds.

Associated Universal Concepts: Destruction

Suspected Location: Neolithic Heart of Orkney, United Kingdom (Confirmed)

Site-01-03:

Predictive Description: Cycles of life and renewal burned deep in the earth on green planes in the depths of steps. Beneath the long-decayed structures of forgotten worshipers of flesh and mother’s pride lies the seals of life’s font forged in the after throes of war.

Associated Universal Concepts: Life/Flesh/Hunger

Suspected Location: Nebelivka, Ukraine (Confirmed)

Site-01-04:

Predictive Description: In rocky hills, far from a once lush green environment, great stones were hewn in the earth, bearing all the symbols of a people noble. Beneath their trappings lies the ticking gears and bronzed metals of an early worship. A sealed machine lays in the depths below, an aspect once captured in rapturous fury with flesh itself.

Associated Universal Concepts: Metal/Machine

Suspected Location: Gobekli Tepe, Turkey (Confirmed)

Site-01-05:

Predictive Description: Five circles of stone in a sunken mass of wells, houses and cairns in an ancient land once ruled by a shepherd king. A great and terrible temple lies beneath where once a great warrior drove her blade into the heart of an unknowable horror. Here lies that which must not be spoken of, that which snakes into the minds of those who know.

Associated Universal Concepts: The Unknown/Unknowable/Hostile Knowledge

Suspected Location: Alit Yam Fifthist Complex, Israel (Confirmed)

Site-01-06:

Predictive Description: Twin cities in a fertile crescent betwixt roaring rivers. Here fire reposes in eternal love. The old gods forged their mark upon the world when the waves swept inland to the west. Upon the ruins of a grand dragon’s body were built the seeds of a civilization. There is where flame lays claim to a seal all its own.

Associated Universal Concepts: Primordial Fire (Flame, Ignition, Burning, Passion/Love/Heat)

Suspected Location: Uruk/Ur, Iraq (Confirmed)

Site-01-07:

Predictive Description: Scarlet dust in a valley of long eroded temples left as stones. Once a mighty lake washed upon the blood-stained sands. In war, the sand was driven south from the grand plateaus of the north where now lies pyramids of stone. Now naught but the rancor of corruption burns beneath a desert sun, biding time for the day of release.

Associated Universal Concepts: Change/Corruption/Power

Suspected Location: Nabta Playa, Egypt (Confirmed)

Site-01-08:

Predictive Description: A grand island at the edge of a ring for fire. Terraces stepping high from fired stone, great sweeping columns towering high. Here beneath the extinction of a mountain the eldest of three was buried in the quenched fires. In times long after the grand terrace arose in monument to mortal ends.

Associated Universal Concepts: Death (Mortality, Decay, Reaping)

Suspected Location: Gunung Padang Megalithic Site, Indonesia (Confirmed)

Site-01-09:

Predictive Description: In once grand savannah cliffs lies an ancient forgotten realm beneath the earth. A temple to transition, to grief deep beneath a village of caves. Here the middle of three was chained to rock and stone in astral whispers.

Associated Universal Concepts: Death (Transition, Finality, Grief)

Suspected Location: Dhar Tichitt, Mauritania (Confirmed)

Site-01-10:

Predictive Description: In the far east along the banks of a fearsome river once lay a palaced city of walls and dams. Here a temple to the youngest of three became a tomb for its essence abandoned amidst overwhelming tides of water.

Associated Universal Concepts: Death (Repose, Acceptance)

Suspected Location: Liangzhu City, China (Confirmed)

Site-01-11:

Predictive Description: (Redacted due to ontokinetic and cognitohazardous effects)

Associated Universal Concepts: Light (Illumination, Cleansing, Contrast)

Suspected Location: Cahokia Illinois, USA (Confirmed)

Site-01-12:

Predictive Description: (Redacted due to ontokinetic and cognitohazardous effects)

Associated Universal Concepts: Darkness (Black, Obscured, Hidden, Absence)

Suspected Location: Sacred City of Caral-Supe, Peru (Confirmed)

Site-01-13:

Predictive Description: (Redacted due to anti-memetic and cognitohazadous effects)

Associated Universal Concepts: Memory/Knowledge

Suspected Location: Sunken Southern Daevic cities of Dwarka and Poompuhar (Confirmed)

Site-01-01:

Predictive Description: In the midst of clear cerulean waters lie the ruins of a wasted and forgotten city of a people of song and beauty. Here in a gratuitous crater beneath the silted sand in a grand cavern lies the beating heart of all Creation. Here she fell from the harsh nothing of the above. Here shall rise into the heavens in the hour the skies become aflame and burn the tyrant below with Astral Fire.

Associated Universal Concepts: Creation (Beginnings, Matter, Earth/Land, Water/Sea, Air/Wind, Cosmology/Space, Creativity/Ideas/Music/Art/Literature, Transformation, Divinity, Birth, Renewal, etc.)

Suspected Location: Chicxulub crater, Yucatan Peninsula, Mexico (Unconfirmed)

Sites which have been identified with SCP-001’s predictions are currently being surveyed for structural stability, thaumatological integrity, conceptual intrusions, and severity of anomalous activity.



Nebelika, Ukraine, Site-001-03.

Addendum 17: New CEO of the Silicon Nornir

Gobekli Tepe, Turkey, Site-001-04.

On 07/18/1975, Speaker Ella Haugen and Speaker Edvard Hägglund passed away due to natural causes. Speaker Ella was CEO of the Cult of the Nornir for 40 years and had been integrated into Omega-77 as SCP-001’s primary contact liaison alongside Speaker Edvard. SCP-001 refused to activate the NFMA for a period of two months. The anomaly was despondent for another six months following its return to activity.

Foundation Diver surveying the Alit Yam submerged surface structures, Israel, Site-001-05.

Speaker Ella’s daughter, Stigurd Haugen, had been in training to take her mother’s place and was promoted to primary contact liaison for SCP-001 and the new head of MTF Omega-77. SCP-001 continues to work with Ms. Haugen, but Omega-77 has observed a notable decrease in the more personalized communication and efficient presentation of prophetic texts and artworks for interpretation that was seen under Speaker Ella.

Ziggurat of Ur, Iraq, Part of the larger structure of Site-001-06.

Update 09/05/1978: Continued efforts to increase the presence of individuals with empathic and strong moral tendencies have increased SCP-001’s communication substantially. New protocols are being implemented to select and train for these qualities.






Addendum 18: Assessment of Major Anomalous Sites and Establishment of Operation High Tide

Director Madeline Light’s Notes, October 8th, 1978:

Pre-1941, we can't find *anything* in the historical record that aligns with SCP-001’s description or depiction beyond maybe some chance similarities to mythological figures.

Calendar Circle at Nabta Playa, Egypt, Part of Site-001-07.

There are some sparse references to historical figures, kings, sultans, great warriors, etc. getting lost on mountainsides and finding oneself in the presence of a goddess of Fate. They read as more fairytale or delusion than realistic history.

We don’t know how old she is, but we can guess based on some of her references.

She’s at least old enough to remember the Bronze Age collapse.

So, this raises the question, why are there not more references to her?

Well, that was the predominant question before we began investigating the major anomalous sites.

On October 8th, 1978, initial evaluations and excavations of the twelve SCP-001 identified major anomalous sites concluded. At least one complex was in relatively good condition considering its age. The other eleven were found to be in varying states of structural decay or outright collapse, which threatened heightened anomalous activity due to poorly understood reasons.

O5-01's office has started sharing photos with me from the sites, which as far as I know, are still remanded to O5 council members. Of course, the Archaeologists have access but other than that…

When he first offered them— I wasn't quite sure why I should be seeing those images, it seemed above my classification level. At first glance I saw what I had seen in GOC files, and other places, and it was immediately weird to me.

Contrary to initial assessments indicating sole involvement of specific ancient anomalous civilizations, all major sites had substantial evidence of Orcadian Culture influence or construction in the use of complex thaumic-tech and engineering (see SCP-3703). The presence and content of unique Orcadian Culture pictographic murals led Foundation researchers to conclude, based on SCP-001’s references, that these were important in some fashion in the impediment of some ancient apocalyptic event.

Orcadian Culture architectural37, thaumatological, thaumic-tech and engineering influences in all of them. It’s bizarre, many of these sites are far far beyond the range of typical Orcadian Culture influence. Or at least, what we thought was the largest extent of their range.

Tourists walking along the surface structure of Gunung Padang, Indonesia, Surface level of Site-001-08.

There are also fragmented texts and tomes and scrolls recovered from these sites and other newly discovered Orcadian Culture sites. They are all in the classical Orcadian script.

We can't decipher it, so it isn't much use to me.

Again, I didn't know why O5-01 had shared any of this with me. It seemed like a major break in classification and information security.

Dhar Tichitt, Mauritania, Site-001-09.

That is until I looked closer at the murals and pictographs.

Right there in front of me, I was looking at a tall feminine figure holding a staff that looked exactly like SCP-001's. There were other symbolic pictographic marks, and thematic associations around her.

What could be a sun.

Threads which seemed like they were literally strings of gold woven into metal.

Scissors, a measuring rod, spools, three silhouettes, snakes, and eyes closed.

I don’t know what any of that means in the context of the Orcadians, specifically. But the staff and similarities of the figure to SCP-001 are difficult to dismiss.

Surface ruins of Langzhou Mega Site, China, Site-001-10.

There's still the possibility of conjecture, but this is the first contextual evidence of what might be SCP-001 outside of modern historical records.

There's a way to know for sure. Something you have to understand about Orcadian Culture artworks is that they are normally incredibly cognitohazardous. There’s really no effective countermeasure to them either. The effects were lost in simple photographs which do not convey the thaumaturgic interface that comes from direct observation.

Cahokia Mound Surface Complex, Illinois, United States, Site-001-11.

These sites are precarious and I don’t have sufficient security access to go see them for myself.

But one day, maybe when I’m an O5, I will go myself, and I will learn the truth.

If anyone would know about SCP-001, it’s the Orcadians.

Who is she?

Sacred City of Caral-Supe, Peru, Site-001-12.

Why is she so present in the minds of the Orcadian Culture?

I can’t ask her yet.

Not until I know more.

She’ll just riddle me to death otherwise.

With these details in mind, O5-01 brought the issue to the O5 council’s attention. The council then voted to establish a new operational command under O5-01’s office, known as Operation High Tide, to begin thorough investigation into the major anomalous sites and the Orcadian Culture.

Poompuhar surface structures, India, One of two coordinating structures for Site-001-13.

SCP-001 Interview Audio and Video Log 151 IVA-001-151:

  • SCP-001 Interview Audio and Log 151 IVA-001-151

Interview Log IVA-001-51

Interviewer: Director Madeline Light.

Date: September 30th, 1980.

Location: Silicon Nornir Central Complex.

Foreword: On September 15th, 1980, the city of Eurtec experienced a large-scale ontological disruption which temporarily disabled all active security measures and most monitoring equipment within the city. SCP-001 was within the NFMA’s Central Focal Interface Chamber at the time of this incident. Bulkhead security doors closed, and it could not be reached by Omega-77 personnel.

When the doors were eventually opened, SCP-001 was found seated in the center of the focal chamber. It was unusually agitated and had to be escorted back to its personal chambers in tower three’s penthouse.

Initially, Omega-77 and Eurtec security personnel believed the cameras within the NFMA focal chamber were affected. However, on September 29th, 1980, Nornir engineers discovered the cameras were still active and pulled the footage for Director Madeline Light to review. Director Light then arranged her normal biweekly interview with SCP-001.

<Begin Log>

Director Light enters the Central Interface Chamber of the NFMA. SCP-001 is standing in the circular area of sunken flooring with stairs in front and behind it. Her hands are slotted into the digital interface, and the screens on the walls of the chamber are lit up with the image of Director Light entering the chamber. The screens abruptly go dark as SCP-001 withdraws its hands.

Director Light: Seeress, how are we today?

SCP-001 turns to the general direction of Director Light.

SCP-001: Madeline, you are early. Most unexpected.

Director Light: Are you seriously telling me you didn’t see me coming? With chocolate milk and candy no less?

SCP-001 waves a hand in the general area of its face.

SCP-001: Nope, nothing.

Director Light laughs.

Director Light: Surely you heard the doors, you can’t be deaf too? Did your hearing get scrambled? Oh, maybe you drank from a tainted pool and got shot through the neck? Am I going to read about sudden changes in your medical chart induced by divine intervention?

SCP-001: The audio quality of the recent dreams has been a bit spotty. I think I need a new aux cord.

Director Light: I’ll see what our doctors can do with such a dated input port.

SCP-001: Are you calling me old?

Director Light approaches and descends the stairs into the pit. She places the pitcher and candy on a table, off to one side with chairs. SCP-001 joins her.

Director Light: Good gods, you really are deaf!

SCP-001: How dare you. I am positively ancient.

Both laugh.

Director Light: Well, you seem in good spirits.

SCP-001: The path continues to wind in favorable ways; there is much to be positive about.

Director Light: Good. That’s what I like to hear. The Councils and Ethics Committee are happy with your work, even if all of them may not agree that we’re using the resources appropriately.

SCP-001: There is still a great deal of work to come. So much to do and so little time.

Director Light: Now you’re making me anxious.

SCP-001: Perhaps soon the dread will sink into your bones, as it does mine.

Director Light: It’s growing every day I look at the reports from the field. It’s still very abstract.

SCP-001: It would be reassuring if I could feel the states of the sites myself.

Director Light: You know exactly why I can’t do that.

SCP-001 grunts.

SCP-001: I expect this will be a short meeting then? Other than the folder in your right hand. I presume that is the most recent list of things the Council wishes to foresee?

Director Light: Well…

Several seconds of silence. SCP-001 tilts its head after drinking from the pitcher of chocolate milk.

SCP-001: I feel tension from you, Madeline. What is it?

Director Light: You’ve lied to us, Seeress.

SCP-001 frowns. Her brow wrinkles.

SCP-001: Madeline, I have not lied to anyone. I never lie about the fate unwinding before us all, only provide what information is most fit and relevant.

Director Light: But you did lie to us very recently.

SCP-001: I’m confused and unsure of what you’re referring to.

Director Light: Let me play a refresher, it might jog your memory.

The following is a transcription of an audio-visual log that was recorded on September 29th, 1980, between the hours of 1200 and 1300 in the NFMA central complex during a major ontological disruption which disabled most monitoring and security systems within the city of Eurtec.

<Begin Log>

SCP-001 is seated within the central focal chamber of the NFMA. The power abruptly fluctuates, and the bulkhead security doors slam shut and seal. SCP-001 stands, visibly startled by the loud noise and sudden disruption to its biometric interfaces.

The lights blink off for several seconds. When they return, there is another figure in the room with SCP-001. They are a middle-aged woman dressed in a well-tailored red pants-suit with brown hair. The face is visible but has antimemetic properties which prevent structural details from being retained. She speaks to SCP-001.

????: Good, I was able to execute and get inside with [Ontological Disrupted Name Removed]’s help. I was concerned this trick might not work. [Additional Ontologically Disrupted Name Removed] it’s good to see you again, in the flesh.

SCP-001 slams the butt of its staff on the floor. Its teeth are bared and it gestures rapidly.

SCP-001: Who are— What are you doing? Why are you—you should not be here! Not now!

????: I needed to make contact. After we sent me back, I needed to be sure that this is it, that we fixed it. Did we do it? You’re not her, but you must have seen it already.

SCP-001: Of course I’ve seen glimpses of that strand. I have lived it, through a thousand, thousand lives. I know what we did, I know whence we sent you too. We didn’t take you to be a rash fool.

????: I’m almost offended by the implications of being called rash. I have spent years constructing dozens of countermeasures to prevent the Jailers from identifying me. And have a dozen more tricks stuffed up my sleeve. This is driven by thousands of hours of consideration and study. Relax [Ontological Disrupted Name Removed], you are not the only one who plans in spans of decades.

SCP-001 scowls.

SCP-001: I plan in spans of centuries. The nerve; were you this arrogant before? This short sighted? You twist fate’s strands without seeing them and have the audacity to tell me to relax? You risk upending everything. We gave you very specific instructions not to make contact.

????: Specific instructions with no termination point. I have spent the last four decades of this life moving pieces into place in preparation to keep [Ontologically Disrupted Names Removed] in play. I’ve been tracking everything you’ve done through informants. The GOC is gone, the Jailers wrapped around your thumb. When will be the proper time if not now? I waited ten years for you to reach out after the GOC fell apart. I waited, and waited, prepared to bring myself and the key players to your side so we could build everything needed for what’s coming. And heard nothing. If not now, then when? When it’s on our doorstep?

SCP-001 shifts through several facial expressions rapidly as it steps up out of the pit.

SCP-001: You had informants?

????: Of course I did, Seeress. A magician always has tricks up their sleeve. Now, I have done everything we discussed before I was sent back. What’s the next step, the next keystone in the line to stop what’s coming? When can you join us to build for the next steps in stopping this catastrophe?

SCP-001: Two turns of ten and five revolutions of the sun. Then and only then will the preparations for your rejoining begin. I will go nowhere; did she not tell you this?

????: What? But I—

SCP-001: The Jailers are the key, the foundations are being laid, and the players are being prepared. You are too early and have thrown wrinkles in the early iterations of my webbing and it may very well tear apart beneath your careless weight. I might not be able to undo the damage.

????: …oh. I—

SCP-001: There is nothing else to say. Go now and pray you haven’t unwound everything I have woven. When the time is near, messages will come. Do not do this again.

The camera and audio recording short out.

<End Log>

Several minutes of silence.

Director Light: So…

SCP-001: …

Director Light: You lied to us.

SCP-001: …

Director Light: You told me two weeks ago, nothing happened while you were in here.

SCP-001: …

Director Light: And yet here I am, having been handed evidence to the contrary. Not only was it not nothing, but you met with the source of the ontological disruption and seemed to know them.

SCP-001: …

SCP-001’s hands tremble.

Director Light: You and I are the only ones who have seen or heard this recording. You remember what you have been saying all along? Every time I have tried to ask about your personal details? Well, trust is a two-way street.

SCP-001: …

Director Light: I have trusted you pretty implicitly until this point, Seeress. For the good of normalcy and all humankind. But this is a pretty major trust breaker. So, I’m going to ask you questions about what we just listened to. I expect honest answers if you want to keep building trust with me. Otherwise, we’re going to have to make some changes.

SCP-001: Okay.

Director Light: Right, so, first of all who was that?

SCP-001 hesitates for several seconds.

SCP-001: That’s not a question I can answer, in full.

Director Light: Why not?

SCP-001: There is ontological and conceptual interference that spans infinitely many branches. The true name has been scraped, drowned and buried.

Director Light: Right…?

SCP-001: For simplicity’s sake, they go by the alias Tilda Moose.

Director Light: I know that name.

SCP-001: I’m sure you do. They’re a member of the Serpent’s Hand.

Director Light: Yet they seemed to be quite intimate with you. How do you know them? Were they Edvard’s contact?

SCP-001 shifts.

SCP-001: I don’t know them and I don’t know.

Director Light: I sense it’s more complicated than that.

SCP-001: Other iterations do know them. Or her. Sometimes him or xe or xer or zer. They and them. Through them, I know her.

Director Light: Multi-versal tree gives me a headache.

SCP-001: It goes away with enough time.

Director Light: Chocolate milk seems to help you.

SCP-001: Imagine what it was like before it was invented.

Director Light: I’d rather not. Alright, why do other iterations of you know her? Are you with the Hand in those branches?

SCP-001: No. Those branches are few and far between.

Director Light: Okay, then how do you know her? Work with me here.

SCP-001: We came to know her at the very end, after the unravelling of reality had begun and all that was left was the Library trafficking refugees to other branches. She was searching for ways to avert the end, if not in those branches, then in others.

Director Light: Two of a kind then?

SCP-001: Of a sort. My counterparts appear to have overestimated her capacity for patient, long-term planning, to a certain point.

Director Light: You plan in centuries.

SCP-001: More or less.

Director Light: Humans rarely have the consistency to plan for five years, much less decades.

SCP-001 tilts its head and consider for several seconds.

SCP-001: …fair point.

Director Light: Right, back on track. She found you in the Library at the end of time, looking to stop this apocalypse. Why is it that she seems to know you in this reality when you claimed you’ve never met? Don’t tell me, she’s prescient too?

SCP-001 frowns and blows air out through its nostrils.

SCP-001: No, she’s a magician, a trickster, an academic thaumaturge. No capacity to see the unwinding strands other than perhaps the ability to divine some small parts of fate.

Director Light: Seeress, please stop trying to tap-dance around me. How does she know you so intimately?

SCP-001: I suppose you’ve come to know me long enough to see through the ruses.

SCP-001 hesitates for several more seconds.

SCP-001: I have only caught glimpses of it, but temporal displacement of the mind was involved.

Director Light stops writing.

Director Light: Did you say—

SCP-001: Yes.

Director Light: Not a joke—

SCP-001: Not a joke.

Director Light: How does that work?

SCP-001: You’re asking a blind hermit, worshipped as a goddess by a Cult who has no experience with temporal engineering, how my counterparts and a magician sent her consciousness and memories back in time?

Director Light rubs her temples.

Director Light: Fair point. Self-answering question, in hindsight.

SCP-001: They used a device, somehow managed to pull it out of the crumbling remains of reality from a place called Site-43.

Director Light stares at SCP-001 for several seconds in silence. She gestures.

Director Light: Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, shame on me. Fool me three times—

Director Light takes several deep breaths and gestures again.

Director Light: Alright, I’m going to have to look into all of that.

SCP-001: Please do. I would like to know too.

Director Light: You mean—okay.

SCP-001’s Artistic Impression.

Director Light takes several deeper breaths while pressing thumb and forefinger to the bridge of her nose.

Director Light: What a batshit crazy concept, who the hell would make a machine that sends someone’s consciousness back in time?

SCP-001: A man named Placeholder McDoctorate.

SCP-001 holds up a hastily drawn portrait of Placeholder McDoctorate.

Director Light: How did you— That was rhetorical.

SCP-001: My answer was not.

Director Light rubs her temples.

Director Light: Ok, back on topic before I burst a blood vessel.

SCP-001: Please do not have a stroke. I have come to enjoy seeing your pretty face.

Director Light: Don’t abuse your prescience too much to daydream over it. Now, please, back on topic.

SCP-001: I am always on topic.

Director Light: Sure you are. So, you sent this Tilda Moose back in time in a different branch reality where she, what, displaced her younger self?

SCP-001: Unlikely.

Director Light: Well, how then?

SCP-001: My understanding is it worked by depositing them in their younger self’s body like a dissociative personality, and they work together. It may explain… the rashness of this Moose if the older one is not driving the body.

Director Light: Right. This is all a hard pill to swallow, Seeress, but I believe you.

SCP-001: I would hope so, this reality is stranger than fiction.

Director Light: It certainly is. Why does she want you to join her? Or rather, why do you need her here?

SCP-001 looks at Director Light for several seconds.

Director Light: Wait, don’t tell me, the apocalypse?

SCP-001: It’s complicated, but yes. For the web to reach its fullest completion in preparation for the catastrophe that will eat reality, Moose must defect from the Serpent’s Hand. Only, she seems to have misinterpreted the construction of how this must come to be.

Director Light: She’s supposed to defect? Well, why not now? Why did you send them away and not bring them to me?

SCP-001: There is an order to the webbing, a precision needed to achieve the outcome that will better us all. Too soon, too late, the web will fall apart. Those pieces that they seek to bring with them would not agree to the defection now, and the necessary pieces and technology to ensure they follow the strands aren’t in place yet. To simplify: It’s too early and they are long lived and slow to trust.

Director Light: I should have thought of that. Temporal manipulation of fate. Madness.

Director Light rubs her temples.

Director Light: You seemed surprised to know there were informants in the GOC. Were you not aware they were feeding information about your predictions to the Hand?

SCP-001: An act in part. I long suspected that there were Hand infiltrators. Planned for it. Though I never confirmed it. I suspected it was Edvard, may his soul rest evenly in Elysium. His reactions and sympathies were always strong.

Director Light: You’re right about Edvard but…Elysium?

SCP-001: I have to keep you guessing about my personage.

Director Light laughs briefly.

Director Light: You are going to drive me insane.

SCP-001: I certainly hope not. I would never inflict such cruelty on another. I would also hate to see you locked in a padded room with rats.

Director Light: These discussions are getting dangerous; you derail me so easily. You didn’t know any of this was going to happen?

SCP-001: Some things elude the strands of fate in prescience. Moose and their pieces are frequent flyers of this domain.

Director Light: Can you elaborate on that?

SCP-001: It is difficult to see them at times, due to their integrity to reality and destiny’s outcome, which they frequently defy.

Director Light: So literal forces of agency. I guess that all makes sense and answers all my questions. If something like this happens again, don’t lie to me about it. My trust won’t afford another break like this.

SCP-001: I understand.

Director Light: Good. I’ll seal these records and bury them under classification; there’s no need for anyone to know about this.

SCP-001’s shoulders drop.

SCP-001: The stars and sands of fate greatly appreciate this, Director.

Director Light: Enough to tell me your name and where you came from?

SCP-001: A day far ahead and yet not so distant.

Director Light: A girl can hope for sooner.

SCP-001: Patience is a virtue, Madeline.

Director Light: Did I tell you that they are giving me a promotion?

SCP-001: No, you didn’t, but I already foresaw it.

Director Light: O5-07.

SCP-001: An impressive feat. Will you still have time for these meetings? I have grown quite fond of your presence.

Director Light: That’s touching, Seeress. I’ve grown fond of yours as well. Worry not. Eurtec is still under my remittance in my new position. I’ll just come with a lot more guards and esoteric countermeasures.

SCP-001: I’d expect nothing less.

Director Light: This also means, when the time is approaching, we can discuss this “Moose’s” defection.

Several seconds of silence pass. Director Light’s expression changes gradually.

Director Light: Oh heaven’s sake. You set this whole thing up this way. So that you could make sure it all happens. Did you bait her? When did you set the bait up for me?

SCP-001 smiles.

SCP-001: I am becoming too transparent for you.

Director Light: Or I’m just getting old and easier to fool.

SCP-001: Perhaps. What about your daughter? Will it be challenging to raise her now that you will be an O5?

Director Light: Sophia is in college now, Seeress. But it won’t be long I feel before she joins the Foundation.

Director Light pauses and frowns briefly.

Director Light: Don’t change the subject, Seeress, you haven’t answered my question.

SCP-001: Time moves too quickly for my ancient bones. She will be a part of the generation who strives to restore our world.

Director Light: So you’ve said many times, Seeress. You’re not going to answer my question, are you?

SCP-001: You already know the answer to it.

Director Light shakes her head while smiling.

Director Light: Before I go, I have one more question.

SCP-001: Of course, Madeline.

Director Light: Who are these critical pieces that Moose went back in time for?

SCP-001: [REDACTED]

Director Light’s expression changes several times in rapid succession.

Director Light: Well, that’s a complication.

SCP-001: They always have been.

<End Log>

Director’s Note: Nothing is ever simple with SCP-001. Machinations within machinations. I was starting to get a little lax with her, but with this most recent event I need to keep a closer eye on her.

My bigger concern at the moment is learning about [REDACTED]. Those are names that are littered throughout the GOC’s records, and I have a distinct feeling that this is going to be a lot messier to set up than I first thought.

Addendum 20: Sapphire and Ruby Project Delineation

O5-07’s Notes:

With all my commitments I haven’t had the chance to sit down and collate all my thoughts and notes and scribbles from the past few years. A major development in understanding who SCP-001 is has finally been placed in my lap. As ever with her, it’s filled with twists and complications. I’m going to update her file now with all my notes and the relevant summaries.

Breakthrough in Translations and the Archaeological Sites:

We discovered the Rosetta Stone for the Orcadian Culture with the Phoenicians. SCP-001 led us right to it.

On October 8th, 1980, a major breakthrough in the translation of Proto-Nordic-like runic script38 came through the discovery of a collection of 15th century BCE Phoenician Tablets in the archaeological ruins of Tyre. The tablets consisted of the collation of direct translations of the runic scripts into Phoenician.

Direct translations! An unbelievable find that completely cracked open the entire field of Occultic Archaeology.

The Phoenician tablets revealed the language’s name to be “Elder Finnfelk”. The discovery of the tablets was prompted by a prediction from SCP-001 in early 1980, which prompted Foundation intervention in Tyre’s excavation.

1981, they started decoding everything. Fragments of Scrolls, tablets, metal engravings. Tens of thousands of previously indecipherable materials. We started to get glimpses into the world of this mysterious civilization.

The Phoenicians referred to the people of the Orcadian Culture as the “Finnfolk” or “The Sea Peoples” providing further clarity on previous Phoenician records.

It wasn’t the most creative of names. But it provided immediate clarity on the context of Mediterranean Bronze Age references.

The Phoenicians descriptions of the Finnfolk largely match the tens of thousands of individual remains recovered by Foundation Archaeologists from other Finnfolk sites, thaumatological relics and remains discovered within major anomalous sites of concern (see SCP-3703 and SCP-3706). This complicating clarification also provides an immense wealth of new understanding of other Bronze Age Mediterranean records. At the same time the Foundation is now scrambling to begin the process of searching for other texts, Phoenician or otherwise, which would need to be seized and/or altered to obscure this history of the civilization from the public.

Most of the initial texts were too fragmented and devoid of their context to be of much worth beyond linguistic purposes. What we did have, early in the process, that did have context was mostly logistical or technical in nature. Sometimes mixed in with these were other bits and pieces of insight into the Finnfolk’s greater culture and small daily rituals, and there were at least a few smaller texts purely dedicated to such things. Even those texts were rarely in condition to derive greater context and those that were, were few and far between. Additional context has been given by the cultures that interacted with them which has recontextualized a great deal of what we thought we knew. But there’s still a tremendous gap in knowledge.

With that said, the more texts that were deciphered, the more enthralled I have become. As we’ve been reevaluating almost everything and translating Finnfolk materials, SCP-001’s presence has become increasingly apparent.

Sumerian Mural of SCP-001 and Ereshkigal.

Most of the materials from mundane bronze age civilizations or their precursors refer to SCP-001 by specific titles. The Egyptians referred to her as the Weaver. The Sumerians referred to her as the “Thresher”, or bringer of fate. She was frequently depicted as a foreign companion of the Sumerian Queen of the Underworld, Ereshkigal.

Where they didn’t have a specific title for SCP-001, it seems they often misidentified her as a goddess within their pantheon. The Hittites referred to her as Istustaya or Papaya.39 Most of these references were in passing and rarely went into detail beyond chance mentions. It wasn’t until I was forwarded a translation from a 17th century BCE tablet that I truly became certain these references were to SCP-001.

Phoenician Excerpt referring to SCP-001:

…In the sweet summer Meni came down from atop her mountain far in the north and west. She was brilliant as Astarte, with whom our forebears once saw her travel the many seas. Brown of hair, her eyes the burst of sun kissing against my skin. She held a grand staff ending in some strange golden instrument, and her eyes, despite their beauty, had been deprived of the sight of the great heavens above. She stood tall above, strands of destiny woven in her hair as she walked. A darkness clung to her, cast by the light which radiated from her flesh.

The people of the sea came for her, and she sailed to the west. We had good fortune in fish that year.

There’s a certain irony in these mistaken impressions given the current circumstances. I’m sure the actual divinities were somewhat perturbed by the assertion.

The one place where SCP-001’s presence seems to be somewhat absent or diluted is the Mycenaeans and the Minoans. Then again, we haven’t been able to decipher Linear A, due to a sheer lack of texts or corresponding cultural cross references with their language. With that said, a better way to look at the influence of SCP-001 on these civilizations is to look at Greek Mythology. Given the Oracles and the number of prescient figures within Greek Myths, it’s possible that SCP-001 inspired multiple different iterative depictions that fragmented in a centuries long game of telephone. Then they propagated further with multiple iterations across different city states. My personal theory is that SCP-001 is likely the prophet Tiresias or possibly some reincarnation of the Moirai who isn’t bound to the underworld. Then again, she may be something else entirely.

Someday I will get to the truth.

Whatever the case may be, all references to SCP-001 in mundane texts ceased in roughly 1200 BCE.

In terms of Finnfolk texts and inscriptions, SCP-001’s description appeared many times in translated materials which have been deciphered. These texts can all be dated to ~3050-3000 BCE. The very first appearance of her description came from a tablet dated to 3050 BCE.

In the name of our people, I was sent to the south, avoiding the great earthworks of the land of Dogger40 to bring my vessel to a grotto I knew well. That morning it was heavy with mist and ominous intent that could not be placed. Why I had come this far at the word of the house of Aquailian? I could not say, but I had heard a voice in my sleep and saw the swirls of creation dancing out before me.41 So, I went forth to see what was required.

There in that grotto there was a woman of muddy flesh42 and tired posture. She was tall for one of them. Her eyes were intense but hooded. They reflected the burst of the dazzling sun hidden beneath the greying mist above our heads. It was not until seconds later that I realize she was blind, for I thought and felt as if she was staring straight through me. In her proximity I felt as if my lifeforce was being strummed, strung in shortening arcs across a coral harp like one of the gristle instruments of the south. She had a weight to her, a darkness which draped me in purple shadow like the tenth. Yet a gentleness like the warm kiss of the Sixth’s flame in her speech. Just standing there I knew, she knew, when I would leave this world for the boundless paradise of the beyond in the way her eyes softened. I don’t know how I know and I’m not sure I ever care to learn.

I don’t recall what she said to me. I don’t know if she ever spoke. It was such a feverish dream that I don’t remember her stepping foot in my skiff, only that she was suddenly seated behind me. I don’t think I heard her voice, but yet I knew it was soft and quiet and filled with the weight of a hard life I could barely begin to understand. Next I knew, we were set sail towards the North. There I dropped her on the isle of angered storms and knew our fate was in her hands.

This text indicates that SCP-001 is a bit more than 5000 years old, possibly older, confirming the suspicions of both the GOC and the Silicon Nornir.

After 2950 BCE, all references appeared to cease.
